info.title: Claude Messages API
info.description: Anthropic-compatible Messages API, exposed through AIsa. Send structured messages and generate responses using Claude models.

BearerAuth.description: Your AIsa API key as a Bearer token.

post.description:
Send a structured, multi-turn conversation to a Claude model and get its response. Compatible with the Anthropic Messages API; supports tool use, extended thinking, streaming, and fine-grained sampling controls.

**Error status codes:** `400`, `401`, `429`, `500`

max_tokens.description: Upper bound on how many tokens the model may generate before it is forced to stop. The model can stop earlier on its own.

messages.description: The ordered conversation turns. Alternate user and assistant turns to supply prior context; the model continues from the last turn.

role.description: Who authored the turn, distinguishing user input from prior assistant output.

content.description: The turn's content, given either as plain text or as an array of structured content blocks (for example text plus images or tool results).

system.description: A top-level system prompt that frames the model's behavior across the whole conversation, given as text or as an array of text blocks.

temperature.description: Controls output randomness; lower values make replies more focused and deterministic, higher values make them more varied. Tune either this or `top_p`, not both.

top_p.description: Nucleus sampling: the model samples only from the most probable tokens whose cumulative probability reaches this threshold. Use as an alternative to `temperature`.

top_k.description: Limits sampling to the K most likely tokens at each step, removing long-tail low-probability options.

stop_sequences.description: Custom text sequences that, once produced, immediately halt generation.

stream.description: Whether to stream the response incrementally over server-sent events instead of returning it all at once.

tools.description: The set of tools the model is allowed to call during the turn.

name.description: The tool's name, by which the model refers to it when calling.

description.description: Explanation of what the tool does, which the model reads to decide when and how to call it.

input_schema.description: JSON Schema describing the arguments the tool accepts, used to validate and shape the model's tool calls.

type.description: The kind of tool, distinguishing a custom-defined tool from other supported tool types.

tool_choice.description: Governs whether and how the model is allowed to invoke the supplied tools on this turn.

type.description: The tool-use strategy: let the model decide, require it to use some tool, force a specific named tool, or forbid tool use entirely.

name.description: The specific tool to force, used when the strategy targets a single named tool.

disable_parallel_tool_use.description: When set, restricts the model to one tool call at a time instead of issuing several in parallel.

thinking.description: Enables extended thinking, giving the model room to reason internally before answering complex problems.

type.description: Switch that turns extended thinking on.

budget_tokens.description: Token allowance set aside for the model's internal reasoning.

display.description: How the thinking trace is surfaced in the response: returned as a summary, or omitted from the output.

user_id.description: An opaque identifier for the end user driving the request; must not contain personally identifiable information.

metadata.description: Optional request metadata used for tracking and analytics.

service_tier.description: Selects the service tier for the request, trading off automatic capacity selection against pinning to standard capacity only.

id.description: Unique identifier of the generated message.

role.description: Author role of the response, the assistant.

content.description: The response body as a sequence of content blocks, which may include text, tool-use requests, or thinking output.

model.description: The model that produced the response.

stop_reason.description: Why generation ended: a natural end of turn, hitting a stop sequence, reaching the token cap, or pausing to call a tool.

stop_sequence.description: The specific stop sequence that triggered the stop, when generation ended for that reason.

cache_creation_input_tokens.description: Input tokens used to write new entries into the prompt cache.

cache_read_input_tokens.description: Input tokens served from the prompt cache rather than recomputed.

usage.description: Token accounting for the request, including prompt-cache reads and writes.
